explanations.1: This declares a section called .data where we store fixed data like strings and numbers that don't change while the program runs.
explanations.2: We're creating a label 'msg' that points to the text 'Hello, world!' followed by a newline character (0Ah); the 0 at the end marks where the string ends.
explanations.3: This declares a section called .text where we write the actual instructions our CPU will execute.
explanations.4: We're telling the assembler that _start is a global symbol, meaning the operating system can find and run our program starting from this label.
explanations.6: We're putting the number 13 into the edx register, which tells the system call how many characters to print (the length of 'Hello, world!').
